quick_facts.label: Level and focus
quick_facts.value: M.F.A. is a postgraduate fine-arts pathway. At JNAFAU, it is offered in discipline-specific streams including Applied Art & Visual Communication, Painting, and Sculpture.

quick_facts.label: Typical eligibility
quick_facts.value: Eligibility is commonly tied to a relevant B.F.A. specialisation or equivalent. For example, JNAFAU lists B.F.A. qualifications matched to the MFA discipline, while the University of Delhi requires a BFA in the same relevant specialisation for its MFA admission route.

quick_facts.label: Typical duration
quick_facts.value: Two years is a common full-time structure: JNAFAU lists two years for its MFA programmes, and Visva-Bharati's Painting MFA syllabus specifies two years across four semesters.

quick_facts.label: Learning format
quick_facts.value: The programme can combine advanced studio/project work with contextual practice, new-media or installation work, presentation-based assessment, and a dissertation. Exact content depends on the university and specialisation.

sections.heading: Eligibility: normally a relevant B.F.A., with specialisation matching
paragraphs: Entry requirements are set by each university rather than being identical nationwide. A representative current institutional pattern is admission after a relevant B.F.A. or equivalent: JNAFAU specifies discipline-matched qualifications such as B.F.A. Applied Art for MFA Applied Art & Visual Communication, B.F.A. Painting for MFA Painting, and B.F.A. Sculpture for MFA Sculpture. For its MFA route, the University of Delhi likewise specifies a BFA in the same relevant specialisation from a recognised university.

Applicants should therefore check whether a university accepts only the matching studio discipline, permits an equivalent qualification, imposes minimum marks, or asks for evidence of prior practice. These conditions can differ even between MFA specialisations at the same institution.
bullets: A B.F.A. is the usual immediate feeder qualification.
Matching the B.F.A. specialisation to the proposed MFA stream is often required.
An “equivalent” qualification may be accepted only where the university expressly provides for it.

sections.heading: Duration: commonly two years / four semesters
paragraphs: A two-year full-time MFA is a well-established institutional format, but applicants should rely on the prospectus for the programme they intend to join. JNAFAU lists two years for its MFA offerings; Visva-Bharati's Painting MFA curriculum expressly states a two-year, four-semester course. The precise calendar, credit load, and maximum time permitted for completion are university-specific.
bullets: Confirm the applicable duration in the admission notice and programme regulations of the chosen institution.

sections.heading: Curriculum: advanced studio practice with research and presentation
paragraphs: MFA curricula are built around the selected art discipline rather than a single national syllabus. Visva-Bharati's MFA Painting syllabus illustrates the character of advanced work: advanced drawing, composition, interdisciplinary projects, multimedia or artist-book projects, new media, installation, self-reflective contextual practice, and dissertation components. Assessment in that example includes internal work and examination or presentation.

This makes the programme particularly suited to artists seeking structured time, critique, production facilities, and guided development of a coherent body of work. Applicants should compare departmental syllabi, studios, media facilities, faculty expertise, and the final assessment requirements—not just the degree title.
bullets: Studio and project work may be central.
Interdisciplinary, multimedia, or installation-based practice may be included.
A dissertation, synopsis presentation, and/or final project can form part of assessment.

sections.heading: Admission: entrance-based processes are common, but differ by university
paragraphs: Admission mechanisms vary by institution and cycle. In the University of Delhi's MFA process described for CSAS (PG) 2026, applicants must meet programme-specific eligibility, take the relevant CUET (PG) paper, and apply for a practical-based test; merit is calculated using 50% CUET (PG) score and 50% practical-test score. JNAFAU lists a University Entrance Test for its MFA streams, and its 2025 MFA notice described written/practical assessment and interview elements, with a portfolio component for Photography & Media Communication.

Applicants should treat entrance subjects, portfolios, practical tests, interview requirements, seat counts, reservation provisions, application dates, and document lists as annual institutional rules. Consult the official admission portal for the relevant intake rather than relying on a prior-year notice.
bullets: Possible selection components: entrance examination, practical test, interview, and portfolio.
University of Delhi example: CUET (PG) plus practical-based test.
JNAFAU example: university entrance testing; the 2025 notice also detailed interviews and, for one stream, a portfolio.

sections.heading: Costs: institution-specific; budget beyond tuition
paragraphs: There is no single national MFA fee. As a dated example only, JNAFAU's 2025 admission information for its self-financed MFA streams listed tuition of ₹75,000 per annum and stated that ₹5,000 infrastructure fee and ₹100 green fund were additionally payable each year; the notice also indicated equipment expectations for some streams. This should not be used as a forecast for later cycles or other universities.

Before applying, obtain the current official fee notice and budget for tuition, one-time deposits, studio or laboratory charges, materials, equipment, printing, exhibition or portfolio production, accommodation, and travel. At studio-intensive programmes, materials and equipment can be a meaningful separate expense.
bullets: Published tuition is not necessarily the full cost of attendance.
Ask whether additional fees apply for infrastructure, deposits, library, materials, equipment, or specialist facilities.
Use the current admission-cycle notice for the final payable amount.

sections.heading: After the MFA: doctoral research is a formal academic progression option
paragraphs: An MFA holder may pursue doctoral research where they meet the applicable university and UGC requirements. The UGC Ph.D. Regulations, 2022 provide that candidates with a two-year/four-semester master's degree after a three-year bachelor's degree, or a one-year/two-semester master's degree after a four-year bachelor's degree, may seek Ph.D. admission with at least 55% aggregate marks or an equivalent grade; specified category relaxations may apply. Admission remains subject to the institution's notified criteria.

For applicants planning an academic or research route, the MFA's dissertation and contextual-practice components can help develop research-writing and proposal skills, but they do not replace a Ph.D. entrance process or institution-specific eligibility review.
bullets: Possible formal progression: Ph.D. study in an appropriate art, visual-culture, design, or interdisciplinary research area.
UGC's cited baseline includes a master's degree and 55% aggregate or equivalent grade, subject to stated relaxations and institutional admission criteria.
Verify the target university's Ph.D. discipline, supervisor availability, entrance process, and portfolio or research-proposal requirements.
